assessment_items.question_content:
Which is NOT referenced in this painting by Robert Motherwell? 


![Robert Motherwell, Elegy to the Spanish Republic No. 57, 1957–60, oil on canvas, 84 x 109-1/8 inches (San Francisco Museum of Modern Art)](${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/6d63c700e8c2f5d3ead1407221bfe13a.jpeg)

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints:
Inspired by poems by Harold Rosenberg and Federico Garcia Lorca, Motherwell wanted to find an abstract visual language to express his own personal response to The Spanish Civil War.  

Image shown: Robert Motherwell, *Elegy to the Spanish Republic, No. 57*, 1957-1960 (SFMOMA)

choices.content: traditions of the female nude in art history
a poem by Federico Garcia Lorca
the artist's personal response to human suffering

assessment_items.question_content:
**In understanding Abstract Expressionist paintings of the 1950s and 60s, the viewer should focus on . . .**



![Helen Frankenthaler, The Bay, 1963, acrylic on canvas, 204.2  x 208.6 x 2.2 cm (Detroit Institute of Arts)](${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/935f680cbd2dd589ad61069e6b02b995.jpg)

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints:
**With many Abstract Expressionist paintings of the 1950s and 60s, it’s important not to get too caught up with possible social and historical contexts and biography, but to focus on what’s before us—the physical elements of the work itself because those elements can tell us so much about the painting.**

**[Read this article to review](/humanities/art-history/art-history-1907-1960-age-of-global-conflict/American%20Modernism/a/frankenthaler-the-bay)**

choices.content: the physical elements of the work.

assessment_items.question_content:
Which painting is a good example of the Color Field style of Abstract Expressionism?

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints: Some Abstract Expressionists emphasized their creative process through bold gestural brushwork in a style known as Action Painting, whereas others, known as Color Field painters, applied broad expanses of color to create meditative images that evoked a spiritual sublime.

choices.content: ![Barnett Newman, Onement, I, 1948, oil on canvas, 69.2 x 41.2 cm (The Museum of Modern Art, New York)](${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/cd18755a53ae76c1cd210e1e92b630c0.jpeg)

![Willem de Kooning, Woman, I, 1950–52, oil on canvas, 192.7 x 147.3 cm (The Museum of Modern Art, New York) © The Willem de Kooning Foundation](${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/e0c83490e6fb3a444df79dd325e301f8.jpeg)

![Robert Motherwell, Elegy to the Spanish Republic No. 57, 1957–60, oil on canvas, 84 x 109-1/8 inches (San Francisco Museum of Modern Art)](${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/e2f0a9b8bd29ed38fe310f0c9dc5b5e5.jpeg)

![Jasper Johns, Flag, 1954–55 (dated on reverse 1954), encaustic, oil, and collage on fabric mounted on plywood, three panels, 107.3 x 153.8 cm (The Museum of Modern Art, New York)](${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/5f193e7b724c93775fc270e13dc059e3.jpeg)
